description: Next.js landing page with animated terminal demo, how-it-works section, and download CTAs

userStories.title: Layout, fonts, and global CSS custom properties
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the root layout with correct fonts and CSS variables so all components use the design system.
acceptanceCriteria: landing/app/layout.tsx imports Google Fonts: Syne (700, 800), Azeret Mono (400, 500), DM Sans (300, 400, 500)
Global CSS defines all custom properties: --bg (#07070A), --bg2, --bg3, --border, --border-mid, --text, --text-muted, --text-dim, --accent (#00E676), --accent-flat, --amber, --red, --blue
html and body set to --bg background, --text color, font-family DM Sans
No Tailwind utility classes override the design system colors — use CSS variables
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Hero section with animated entry
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to immediately understand what Auto-Issue does through a bold headline and staggered entrance animation.
acceptanceCriteria: Grid background with --border lines every 48px and radial mask fading at the bottom
Badge at top: pulsing dot + 'Open source · Growthway Hack 2026' text
Label above title: [ AUTONOMOUS CODING AGENT ] in Azeret Mono 11px, --text-muted, letter-spacing 0.14em
Title in Syne 800: 'GitHub Issues that' (line break) 'ship themselves' — 'ship themselves' in --accent, flat, no glow
Subtitle in DM Sans: 'Label an issue. An AI agent writes the code, runs the tests, and opens a PR.'
Staggered fade-up animation: badge (0ms) → title (100ms) → subtitle (200ms) → CTAs (300ms) → terminal (400ms)
All animations use CSS keyframes only — no Framer Motion, no GSAP
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Download CTA buttons
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want clear download buttons so I can install Auto-Issue on my OS.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary CTA: 'Download for macOS →' with --accent background and dark text
Secondary CTA: 'View on GitHub' ghost style with --border border and --text text
Below buttons: smaller text links for Windows and Linux downloads
All download links point to GitHub Releases URLs (placeholder hrefs for MVP)
Buttons are horizontally centered below the subtitle
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Terminal animation — 22-second loop
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see a simulated terminal showing an AI agent resolving a GitHub issue.
acceptanceCriteria: Terminal component with titlebar (traffic lights + label), log area, and status bar
Exact 10-line log sequence with specified delays from 0ms to 7800ms
Each line appears with fade-in + translateY animation (CSS keyframes)
Status bar starts as 'running' with purple pulsing dot, changes to 'done · PR #87 opened' (green) on last line
After 3 seconds on done state, terminal clears and restarts the loop
Total cycle approximately 22 seconds
Uses setTimeout chain with clearTimeout on cleanup — no memory leaks
Log colors: INFO→--text-muted, T·NN→--text (white), OK→--accent, DONE→--accent weight 500
Font: Azeret Mono 13px, background --bg2, border --border-mid
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: How it works — 3-step section
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to understand the product workflow in 3 simple steps.
acceptanceCriteria: 3-column grid separated by --border vertical lines, no elevated cards
Step 1 — [ 01 ] Label the issue: tag SVG icon, description with inline code snippets
Step 2 — [ 02 ] Agent takes over: spark SVG icon, description about isolated workspace
Step 3 — [ 03 ] Review the PR: checkmark SVG icon in --accent, description about auto-commit
Step numbers use bracket pattern [ 01 ] in Azeret Mono
Text uses DM Sans for descriptions
Responsive: stacks vertically on mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Final CTA section
userStories.description: As a visitor who has scrolled through the page, I want one more prompt to take action.
acceptanceCriteria: Top border --border with subtle radial purple glow in background
Label: [ GET STARTED ] in Azeret Mono muted
Title in Syne 800: 'Ready to stop supervising agents?'
Subtitle: 'Connect your repository and watch the first PR open itself.'
Two buttons: 'Download for macOS →' (primary) + 'View on GitHub' (ghost)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Footer
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a minimal footer with project attribution.
acceptanceCriteria: Top border --border, vertical padding 1.5rem
Left side: 'auto-issue · growthway hack 2026' in Azeret Mono muted
Right side: 'built with Go + Next.js' in Azeret Mono muted
Responsive: stacks on mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
